title: exocarpos vitiensis
intro: Exocarpos vitiensis, also known as the Fijian quandong or Viti quandong, is a shrub native to the Pacific island of Fiji, belonging to the family Santalaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating various health issues such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing and immune system support. Exocarpos vitiensis can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine.
